overview.short: The Sri Ranganthaswami Temple in Nellore, Andhra Pradesh, India is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Ranganatha a resting form of Lord Vishnu.
overview.description: The Sri Ranganthaswami Temple in Nellore, Andhra Pradesh, India is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Ranganatha a resting form of Lord Vishnu. This temple, also called Talpagiri Ranganathaswami temple or Ranganayakulu is one of the oldest temples in Nellore. It is located on the banks of the Penna River and is believed to have been constructed in the 12th century. Just before the main entrance of the temple is a huge tower, called Gaaligopuram, which literally means "wind tower".

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Talpagiri Ranganadha Swamy Temple, Nellore?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Talpagiri Ranganadha Swamy Temple, Nellore is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Talpagiri Ranganadha Swamy Temple, Nellore?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kadapa Airport (70 km) and the nearest railway station is Tirupati Railway Station (139 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Talpagiri Ranganadha Swamy Temple, Nellore?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Talpagiri Ranganadha Swamy Temple, Nellore at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Talpagiri Ranganadha Swamy Temple, Nellore?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Talpagiri Ranganadha Swamy Temple, Nellore start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
